question: Which papers report more annotators than data sources?
gold_sparql: PREFIX orkgr: <http://orkg.org/orkg/resource/> PREFIX orkgc: <http://orkg.org/orkg/class/> PREFIX orkgp: <http://orkg.org/orkg/predicate/> PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#> SELECT DISTINCT ?paper ?paperLabel WHERE { ?paper orkgp:P31 ?contribution . ?contribution a orkgc:C121001 . ?contribution orkgp:P181031 ?annotationProc . ?annotationProc orkgp:P181032 ?annotator . ?annotator orkgp:P59120 ?numAnnotators . ?contribution orkgp:P181011 ?dataset . ?dataset orkgp:P181017 ?source . ?source orkgp:P181019 ?numSources . FILTER(xsd:decimal(?numAnnotators) > xsd:decimal(?numSources)) OPTIONAL { ?paper rdfs:label ?paperLabel } }

OPTIONAL { ?paper rdfs:label ?paperLabel } }
